title: secretx-gcp-sm: put() with ?version= incompatibility not documented in method
description: What is unclear: the WritableSecretStore::put() method on GcpSmBackend creates a new secret version. When the backend was constructed with ?version=N (a pinned version), subsequent get() calls return version N, not the newly created version. This is a documentation gap visible to a contributor implementing or maintaining the backend — there's no doc comment on the put() implementation warning about this interaction. A contributor who reads only the put() code (not the full module doc) would not know the version/put interaction is broken. What would make it clear: a doc comment on the put() impl saying 'Note: always creates a new version. If this backend was constructed with a pinned ?version=, get() will not return the new version — construct without ?version= for a writable use case.'
